title: Action Surge never recharges on short rest — code comment asserts the opposite of the rulebook
evidence: rests.ts:17 `const ACTUALLY_LONG_REST = new Set(['action_surge'])` and rests.ts:49 `if (ACTUALLY_LONG_REST.has(f.slug)) continue;` exclude action_surge from short-rest recharge. The justifying comment (rests.ts:9 'actually long-rest in 5e RAW') is factually wrong: rules.md §14 lists Action Surge (Fighter) under 'Per Short or Long Rest'. A fighter who uses Action Surge gets it back only on a long rest — a direct nerf the player will hit in every multi-encounter day.
rule_citation: rules.md §14 (Resting/Recharge Triggers — 'Per Short or Long Rest: Action Surge (Fighter)')
recommendation: Delete the ACTUALLY_LONG_REST override and the misleading comment; let action_surge recharge with the other SHORT_REST_RECHARGES entries.
verdict_notes: Confirmed all three cited locations: src/engine/rests.ts:9 (wrong comment), :17 (ACTUALLY_LONG_REST set), :49 (continue skips action_surge in the short-rest recharge loop). data/rules.md §14 line 624 explicitly lists Action Surge under 'Per Short or Long Rest', contradicting the code comment; actual 5e RAW agrees (level 17 changes uses, not recharge trigger). Searched for compensating code: short_rest handler (src/engine/tools/handlers.ts:329-335) calls shortRest() directly with no patch; the meta-tool rest(short_rest) path routes to the same engine function, so the bug manifests identically on the current default vault/qwen3 path; no LLM-facing restore-resource tool exists. The finding is actually WORSE than claimed: src/ai/master/system-prompt.ts:839-840 tells the model Action Surge 'Recharges on short rest (short_rest already handles this)', so the AI master will narrate a recharge the engine never applied, then use_action_surge fails with no_uses_remaining — narration/state desync on top of the rules violation. Tests at tests/engine/rests.test.ts:42-49 and tests/engine/scenarios/full-rest-cycle.test.ts:94-117 intentionally assert the divergent 'Plan B' behavior, so they must be updated alongside the fix (delete the ACTUALLY_LONG_REST override per the recommendation). Severity stays high: deterministic, every-multi-encounter-day nerf to the Fighter's signature feature on the default path, plus an engine/prompt contradiction.

title: Vault begin turn always receives the STRONG combat directive (begin instruction text itself trips detectCombatIntent) — contradicts narration-only opener and reintroduces the HANDOFF 2e.5 contradiction class
evidence: On a vault begin turn, vaultHistory = [buildBeginUserMessage(...)] (route.ts:285), and route.ts:317-319 sets _playerMessage to that begin message text. buildTurnDirective at route.ts:648 has NO isBegin gate. The IT begin instruction (begin-message.ts:22) contains 'NON iniziare un combattimento, NON far attaccare alcuna creatura' and 'se affrontare' — COMBAT_INTENT_RE (turn-directive.ts:78-79) matches attacc\w*/combatt\w*/affront\w*, so detectCombatIntent(_playerMessage) is TRUE on EVERY vault begin (EN text too: 'attack', 'fight'). With vaultMutationsEnabled (e.g. Goblin Warren per HANDOFF §3), serverResolved/monsterResolved both false on begin, so turn-directive.ts:184-194 returns '[ISTRUZIONE PRIORITARIA — il giocatore sta attaccando] PRIMA di narrare l'esito DEVI usare gli strumenti (apply_event): ... chiama combat_start, poi monster_spawn ... Poi chiedi il tiro al giocatore'. appendDirectiveToHistory (route.ts:664) glues it AFTER the begin instruction in the same user message — the recency position the project itself validated as dominant for local MoE models. Meanwhile isNarrationOnlyTurn (combat-resolver.ts:107) forces offerTools:false on begin, so the model is ORDERED to call a tool it does not have, and ordered to open combat/ask a roll that begin-message.ts:22 and commit e874863 explicitly forbid. Any leaked 'apply_event combat_start {...}' / 'Tira 1d20...' text is NOT stripped: the else-branch guard at route.ts:866-873 only strips when the replayed encounter is active (false on a fresh campaign). Lesser variant: vaultMutations=false + manualRolls=true still emits the roll-line directive ('chiedi un tiro al giocatore', turn-directive.ts:236-237) on the begin turn, contradicting 'NON chiedere alcun tiro di dado'.
recommendation: Gate the directive on the opener: skip buildTurnDirective entirely when isBegin (or pass isBegin and return null / a POV-only block). Alternatively, set _playerMessage to undefined on begin turns (the begin message is not a player message). Add a regression test: buildTurnDirective must not return the combat-first form for the BEGIN_INSTRUCTION text in any supported language.
verdict_notes: CONFIRMED by code reading AND empirical reproduction (tsx run of the actual modules). (1) route.ts:279-286 builds vaultHistory=[begin message] on isBegin; route.ts:317-319 sets _playerMessage to that text; route.ts:648 calls buildTurnDirective with NO isBegin gate — while the encounter opener at route.ts:368 IS gated on !isBegin, proving the gate was simply missed for the directive. (2) Empirical: detectCombatIntent(buildBeginUserMessage(...)) = true for IT, EN, FR (false for ES/DE/PT); with vaultMutations=true the returned directive is byte-for-byte the strong '[ISTRUZIONE PRIORITARIA — il giocatore sta attaccando] ... chiama combat_start, poi monster_spawn ... Poi chiedi il tiro al giocatore' — appended after the begin instruction on the same user turn (appendDirectiveToHistory, recency position the project validated as dominant for qwen3). serverResolved/monsterResolved are both false on begin (resolver requires isRollResult; no active encounter), so no suppression applies. (3) isNarrationOnlyTurn (src/app/api/sessions/[id]/turn/combat-resolver.ts:107) returns true on isBegin → offerTools:false → the model is ordered to call apply_event without having it (same contradiction class as the HANDOFF 2e.5 meta_action-as-text bug). (4) The leak-strip at route.ts:866-873 only runs when the replayed encounter is active — false on a fresh begin — so leaked roll-asks/apply_event text persist into the opener. (5) Lesser manualRolls-only variant also reproduced. (6) No compensating code: turn-directive.test.ts has zero begin coverage; no commit after e874863 gates this. SEVERITY downgraded critical→high: (a) on the strict current default path vaultMutations is opt-in default-false and manualRolls default-false → buildTurnDirective returns null on begin, no impact; the bug requires vaultMutations=on (which IS the config of both live test campaigns incl. Goblin Warren, and the post-cutover trajectory); (b) no state corruption possible — offerTools:false means the model cannot actually emit combat_start, and the server opener is !isBegin-gated, so impact is a contradicted/garbage opener + leaked mechanics text, not data loss; (c) the empty-narration retry (route.ts:792, fires on begin) and the client Riprova button bound the stuck-UI worst case. Real, player-visible opener regression that directly undoes commit e874863 at the recency position on combat-enabled campaigns — fix recommendation (gate directive on isBegin or null _playerMessage on begin + regression test) is correct.

title: buildTurnDirective is blind to narration-only gating — orders apply_event tool calls on turns where the route passes zero tools; unmitigated leak path when encounter is inactive
evidence: Gate inventory: offerTools=false iff isWeakToolModel(model) OR isNarrationOnlyTurn (= isBegin || vaultMutations && (encounterActive || isCombatDeclaration || isRollResult || resolverFired || monsterLoopRan)) [route.ts:724-734]; suppressCombatMutations iff resolverFired||monsterLoopRan [route.ts:759]; directive suppression iff serverResolved||monsterResolved [turn-directive.ts:151,184,225]. The directive's suppression set ({resolver, monsterLoop}) is a strict SUBSET of the no-tools set ({begin, encounterActive, combatDeclaration, rollResult, weakModel, ...}), so several turns get an explicit order to call apply_event with tools:[] — e.g. (1) combat DECLARATION turn: offerTools=false via isCombatDeclaration, yet turn-directive.ts:184-194 orders 'chiama combat_start, poi monster_spawn, poi initiative_set' (also factually wrong when the 5v-opener already opened the encounter — buildTurnDirective receives no openerRan signal, route.ts:648-659); (2) roll-result during active combat where resolveCombat returns null (unmatched target, route.ts:500-511): offerTools=false, suppressCombatMutations=false, and turn-directive.ts:157-160 orders 'chiama apply_event con monster_hp_change ... apply_event con turn_advance'. Mitigation only covers active encounters (canonicalizeToHitTarget / stripLeakedMechanics at route.ts:869-872 are inside 'if (_declEnc.active)'); when the opener was skipped by the scatto-guard (unknown bestiary name, route.ts:399-402) the encounter stays inactive, no strip runs, and the model's leaked event-JSON / tool prose persists verbatim as the master message — the same literal-tool-text persistence bug as HANDOFF 2e.5.
recommendation: Pass the computed narration-only decision (and openerRan) into buildTurnDirective and emit a narration-only directive variant (no apply_event re-asks) whenever offerTools will be false. Also run stripLeakedMechanics unconditionally on the vault finalText (not only when encounter.active) so literal tool text can never persist.
verdict_notes: CONFIRMED on independent reading. (1) Gate inventory verified verbatim: isNarrationOnlyTurn (src/app/api/sessions/[id]/turn/combat-resolver.ts:98-116) = isBegin || vaultMutations && (encounterActive || isCombatDeclaration || isRollResult || resolverFired || monsterLoopRan); route.ts:724-734 passes offerTools:false on that set; loop.ts:164 maps it to an empty tool array. buildTurnDirective (route.ts:648-659) receives ONLY serverResolved/monsterResolved — no openerRan, no narration-only flag — and its suppression gates (turn-directive.ts:151, 184, 225) form a strict subset of the no-tools set. So combat-declaration turns get the imperative 'DEVI usare gli strumenti (apply_event): chiama combat_start, poi monster_spawn, poi initiative_set' (lines 184-193) and EVERY resolver-null roll-result turn (including ordinary skill checks) gets the unconditional 'chiama apply_event con turn_advance' (line 160) — all with tools:[]. (2) Stale-state claim verified: vaultSys is built at route.ts:258 BEFORE the opener at 368+, and openerRan is never passed, so the directive's combat_start branch can be factually wrong after the opener fired. (3) Mitigation gap verified: canonicalizeToHitTarget/stripLeakedMechanics run only inside if(_declEnc.active) (route.ts:866-882); when the scatto-guard skips the opener for a non-bestiary target (route.ts:399-402) or on exploration skill checks, the encounter is inactive, no strip runs, and _finalNarration persists verbatim (route.ts:964-965). The loop only strips reasoning preambles, not tool text; no other compensating guard found (recent commit e874863 only hardened BEGIN prose). (4) Impact mechanism is documented in-repo with the PRIMARY model: HANDOFF.md §2e.5 records qwen3 writing the ordered-but-unavailable tool call as literal text that was persisted as the master message and stuck the UI; that was fixed for BEGIN only (tonalMandate:false, route.ts:285), leaving the identical contradiction in buildTurnDirective. qwen3-a3b-instruct is explicitly not weak-tool (baked-models.ts:354-359, '100% tool compliance'), i.e. inclined to obey tool orders. Severity kept HIGH: fires on the current default path (vault + qwen3 + mutations) on routine turns (every skill-check roll, attacks on non-bestiary/NPC targets), with an observed precedent of literal tool-text persistence; capped below critical because tools:[] means no state corruption is possible — it is a player-visible narration-integrity bug, not a data bug. Recommendation in the finding (pass narration-only decision + openerRan into buildTurnDirective; run stripLeakedMechanics unconditionally on the vault finalText) is consistent with the code structure.

title: Vault hp_change bypasses engine damage pipeline: no temp-HP absorption, no unconscious/death-save flow at 0 HP, no wake-on-heal
evidence: The vault reducer applies hp_change as `Math.max(0, Math.min(hp_max, hp_current + delta))` (projector.ts:307-311) and the Postgres leg mirrors it (event-to-engine-mutation.ts:124). The server-resolved monster loop deals PC damage exclusively via `hp_change {delta:-damage}` (monster-turns.ts:220). Nothing on this path: (1) consumes temp_hp first — even though the vault tracks temp_hp and the engine's own damage routine absorbs it (engine/combat/damage.ts:156-165); (2) adds 'unconscious' or starts dying when HP hits 0 — the engine routine does (damage.ts:119-140 emits unconscious + death_save failures on damage at 0); (3) wakes a PC healed from 0 — the legacy applicator resets death saves, drops unconscious and clears stable on heal-from-0 (applicator.ts:165-181), the vault reducer just raises hp_current, leaving stale death_saves/unconscious/dead flags. New campaigns default to masterBackend='vault' + vaultMutations=true (src/campaigns/initial-settings.ts:46-58), so every new player hits this.
rule_citation: data/rules.md §3.17 (lines 255-257: 'HP reaches 0 → fall unconscious; PCs make death saves'; 'Temporary HP: separate pool, lost first'), §3.18 (line 267: 'Damage while at 0 HP = 1 failure'), §3.21 (line 279: 'Healing a creature at 0 HP makes it conscious')
recommendation: Route PC damage/healing on the vault path through the engine semantics: either emit composite events (temp_hp_set + hp_change + condition_add('unconscious') + death-save events) from a server-side damage helper shared with engine/combat/damage.ts, or extend the hp_change reducer (and the PG leg identically) to absorb temp_hp, set unconscious/dying at 0, and reset death saves/unconscious/stable on heal-from-0.
verdict_notes: All cited claims verified by direct reads. (1) Vault hp_change reducer (src/ai/master/vault/projector.ts:304-312) is a bare clamp: no temp-HP absorption, no unconscious/dying at 0 HP, no death-save/unconscious/stable reset on heal-from-0. (2) PG dual-write leg (src/sessions/event-to-engine-mutation.ts:115-127) mirrors the bare clamp. (3) Server-resolved monster loop (monster-turns.ts:215-221) deals PC damage exclusively via hp_change{delta:-damage}; its working-HP copy (route.ts:588) also ignores temp_hp, so the loop's own targeting/KO logic bypasses temp HP too. (4) Engine applyDamage (engine/combat/damage.ts:100-165) does absorb temp HP and emit unconscious/death-save failures, but grep shows its only caller is engine/tools/handlers.ts:207 (legacy baked tool surface) — never invoked on the vault path. (5) Legacy applicator heal-from-0 wake logic (applicator.ts:165-181) is not mirrored in either vault leg. (6) data/rules.md §3.17/§3.18/§3.21 say exactly what the finding cites. (7) initial-settings.ts:46-58 confirms vault+vaultMutations is the default for new campaigns. Compensating code found but insufficient: the vault HAS rules-correct death_save_* reducers (projector.ts:400-457) and PG mirrors, but nothing emits them automatically and the vault prompt-builder/turn-directive contain zero mention of death saves or unconscious — the machinery is orphaned. The D-14 party-down design (monster-turns.ts:427-433, 457-458) does make the most common KO (last live PC downed by the monster loop) a narrated party defeat rather than silence, which is why I downgrade critical→high: no crash/data loss, the single-PC wipe path is intentionally (if rules-deviantly) handled, the temp-HP bypass requires temp HP to have been granted first, and stale wake-on-heal requires LLM-initiated death-state. Remaining real player impact on the default path: temp HP is silently useless against server-resolved monster damage; a PC downed outside the loop (trap/fall via LLM hp_change) or a non-last downed PC in a multi-PC party enters a stateless limbo (no unconscious, no dying clock); heal-from-0 via hp_change leaves stale death_saves/unconscious/stable flags.

title: apply_event accepts campaign_initialized from the LLM (mid-campaign state reset) and never checks payload.character against the campaign roster
evidence: The dispatcher validates only shape: validateEvent accepts 'campaign_initialized' with any characters array (events-schema.ts:552-641), and the NIT-1 UUID guard explicitly skips it (tools.ts:311). replayEvents handles a seed by overwriting the state map: `states.set(c.id, INITIAL_CHARACTER_STATE(c))` (projector.ts:929) — so an LLM-emitted seed mid-campaign resets every listed character to full HP / empty conditions / empty inventory, or fabricates new characters; the event is permanent in append-only events.md. Separately, for mutation events the guard only checks UUID SHAPE (tools.ts:313), not membership: an invented-but-valid UUID is appended to events.md, then regenerateCharacterView throws AFTER the append (projector.ts:1032-1035), returning an error to the LLM while the zombie event persists forever (single-write path tools.ts:401-409 has no rollback). On the dual-write path the PG leg silently no-ops for unknown ids (event-to-engine-mutation.ts:123).
rule_citation: logical-consistency (LLM trust boundary — mission item a)
recommendation: Reject 'campaign_initialized' at the dispatcher when it arrives via the LLM tool surface (allow it only from the flip/genesis scripts), and validate payload.character against the replayed seed roster (or a cached roster) BEFORE appending to events.md.
verdict_notes: Verified all three cited files line-by-line; every technical claim holds. (1) events-schema.ts:552-641 validates campaign_initialized shape only — no roster check. (2) tools.ts:311 explicitly exempts it from the NIT-1 UUID guard. (3) projector.ts:926-931 replays a mid-stream seed by overwriting states.set(c.id, INITIAL_CHARACTER_STATE(c)) — an LLM-emitted seed mid-campaign resets every listed character (full HP unless hp_current supplied, wiped conditions/inventory/slots) and nullifies their prior history; regenerateAffectedViews (projector.ts:1093-1098) rewrites the views synchronously so the player sees the reset immediately. The append (tools.ts:403) precedes regen with no rollback — permanent in append-only events.md; recovery requires manual file surgery + rebuild-views. Searched for compensating guards and found NONE: loop.ts dispatches structured tool calls unfiltered except the suppressCombatMutations encounter-drop (loop.ts:402-422); the inline-marker recovery path only allows ENCOUNTER_EVENT_TYPES (loop.ts:306) so leaked text can't trigger it — but a structured tool call goes straight through; route.ts gates apply_event on vaultMutationsEnabled without type filtering. The tool description omits campaign_initialized from its 32-type list, but input_schema.type is an unconstrained string. Aggravating factor the finding missed: read_vault_multi root-routing (tools.ts:233-242) serves campaigns/<id>/events.md, whose first line is a complete campaign_initialized envelope the model can read and mimic. Production genesis writes via EventsWriter directly (seed-vault.ts:177), so the dispatcher accepting this type serves only test fixtures (tools.test.ts seeds through it) — pure attack surface in production. The secondary zombie-UUID claim is also accurate (tools.ts:313 shape-only check; append-then-throw at projector.ts:1032-1035; PG no-op at event-to-engine-mutation.ts:123) but low-impact alone: replayEvents skips unseeded ids with a warning (projector.ts:936-945), so the orphan line is inert junk, not state corruption. Severity: kept high. This fires on the CURRENT default path (vault backend, qwen3 — a model whose identifier hallucinations are documented in tools.ts's own comments). Per-turn likelihood is low (model must emit an undocumented type with valid characters[] shape), but impact is the worst in the category: irreversible, player-visible party-state reset plus silent PG divergence on dual-write campaigns, and the fix is a one-line dispatcher rejection. If weighted purely on expected frequency, medium would be defensible; irreversibility plus zero legitimate use of the type on the LLM surface justify high. No D&D rule citation involved (trust-boundary finding), so rules.md check not applicable.

title: Empty-narration retry re-runs the full vault loop and can double-apply LLM-dispatched apply_event mutations
evidence: route.ts:792: 'if (!vaultResult.finalText.trim() && _resolver === null && !_monsterLoopRan && !openerRan) { ... vaultResult = await runVaultToolLoop(_vaultLoopInput); }'. The gate only excludes SERVER-side combat emissions; it never checks vaultResult.toolCallCount. On a tools-offered turn the first loop pass can successfully dispatch LLM apply_event mutations (loop.ts:429 dispatchVaultTool → events.md persisted, e.g. hp_change -5, spell_slot_use, inventory_remove) and still end with empty finalText (model emits tool calls then no text — the documented gemma/qwen failure mode this retry was added for). The retry then replays the identical input; the model, seeing the same history, re-emits the same apply_event calls → the damage/slot/item mutation is applied TWICE to events.md. The events are append-only with fresh UUIDs (tools.ts:324-330), so nothing deduplicates them.
recommendation: Extend the retry gate with vaultResult.toolCallCount === 0 (a pass that dispatched mutations is not a 'genuine empty' — treat it like the combat-state-changed branch: emit {type:'state'} silently). Alternatively pass offerTools:false on the retry pass.
verdict_notes: CONFIRMED mechanism, severity downgraded high→medium. Verified: (1) route.ts:792 retry gate checks only server-side signals (_resolver/_monsterLoopRan/openerRan); vaultResult.toolCallCount is returned by runVaultToolLoop (loop.ts:454) but never referenced in route.ts — no guard against a first pass that dispatched LLM mutations. (2) Replay is byte-identical: _vaultLoopInput is a reused const; the loop copies history internally (loop.ts:175 'messages = [...history]') so pass 2 has no record of pass 1's tool calls and can re-emit the same apply_event calls. (3) No dedupe: tools.ts:324-330 builds a fresh randomUUID envelope per dispatch; EventsWriter.applyEvent (tools.ts:403) appends to events.md. (4) Empty-finalText-after-mutations is a reachable loop outcome (no_tool_calls+no-text break at loop.ts:304/334, timeout at 221, tool-cap at 368), and qwen3 emitting tool calls with empty content is documented in the route's own begin-turn comment. HOWEVER, compensating guards narrow the blast radius substantially, which the finding underweights: isWeakToolModel (baked-models.ts:354) forces offerTools:false on EVERY turn for gemma — the exact model the retry was added for (commit 602c22e) — so toolCallCount is always 0 there; and isNarrationOnlyTurn (combat-resolver.ts:98-116) strips tools on ALL combat-adjacent turns (begin, encounter-active, declaration, roll-result, resolver, monster), so the high-mutation combat path cannot double-apply. The exposed window on the current default path (vault+qwen3-instruct, mutations on) is only a NON-combat exploration turn where qwen3-instruct dispatches apply_event (potion heal, trap hp_change, inventory/xp) AND ends with empty narration — an intermittent conjunction of two uncommon events. When it fires the corruption is silent and persisted in the append-only source-of-truth log (only fixable by hand-editing events.md), so it is a genuine player-impacting bug, but frequency is low → medium. Recommended fix is correct and cheap: add vaultResult.toolCallCount === 0 to the retry gate, or pass offerTools:false on the retry pass.

title: Weak-tool models (gemma*) get offerTools:false on EVERY turn while the system prompt mandates tool calls — mutations-enabled campaigns on gemma can never mutate non-combat state, and prompt promises tools the dispatcher never provides
evidence: route.ts:724: isWeakToolModel(vaultMasterModel) alone forces offerTools:false unconditionally (every turn, combat or not). But buildVaultSystemPrompt is built independently of that flag (route.ts:258-274) and still ships: 'your FIRST action is to read /tools/index.md ... use any of the 4 listed tools directly' (prompt-builder.ts:337-338), the apply_event mention (286-288), the full Combat lifecycle block — 'CRITICAL — emit every event by actually CALLING the apply_event tool' (202-251) — and the UUID roster 'Use these UUIDs verbatim in apply_event payload.character' (369). The provider receives tools: [] (loop.ts:164). Consequences: (a) the prompt promises a tool surface the dispatcher never offers — mission item (e); (b) the inline-event recovery fallback is disabled exactly here (loop.ts:305 requires offeredTools.length > 0), and the route strips leaked text only when an encounter is active (route.ts:869), so leaked '**hp_change** {...}' prose on non-combat turns is persisted to the player — note all strip regexes (combat-resolver.ts:380-383, 520-524) cover ONLY encounter event names, never hp_change/inventory_add/xp_award labels nor a one-line 'apply_event: {...}' form; (c) on a vaultMutations campaign run with gemma, NO non-combat state change can ever happen: the LLM has no tools, recovery is off, and the server resolvers (opener/resolver/monster-loop) only cover encounter events — HP loss from traps, inventory, spell slots, XP silently never apply, while the prompt tells the model (and the model tells the player) that they do. gemma4 was never a validated model, but baked-models.ts:354 shows it is an anticipated, selectable configuration.
recommendation: Either bar gemma-family models from mutations-enabled vault campaigns at the settings layer, or build a narration-only variant of the vault system prompt (no tool protocol / combat lifecycle / roster) whenever offerTools will be false for the whole turn, and extend the strip helpers to cover all VAULT_EVENT_TYPES labels and the 'apply_event:' prefix form, applied unconditionally before persistence.
verdict_notes: Every code claim verified by direct reading. (1) route.ts:724 — isWeakToolModel(vaultMasterModel) alone (OR'd with isNarrationOnlyTurn) forces offerTools:false on every turn; baked-models.ts:354-359 matches any /\bgemma/i model. (2) The system prompt is built at route.ts:258-274 with vaultMutations:vaultMutationsEnabled and zero weak-model awareness; buildVaultSystemPrompt (prompt-builder.ts, full read) has no narration-only variant — with mutations on it still ships 'use any of the 4 listed tools directly' (337-338), the apply_event mention (286-288), the full Combat-lifecycle block 'CRITICAL — emit every event by actually CALLING the apply_event tool' (202-251), and the UUID roster (367-374). grep confirms isWeakToolModel appears in exactly two files: route.ts and its definition — no prompt-builder/turn-directive/settings usage. (3) Worse than the finding states: the per-turn recency directive (route.ts:648, turn-directive.ts:225-231) ALSO instructs 'USA apply_event' with the combat catalog on general turns, unaware of the weak-model flag — so the contradiction sits in both the system prompt and the last user turn while loop.ts:164 hands the provider tools:[]. (4) Inline-event recovery confirmed disabled: loop.ts:304-305 requires offeredTools.length>0; it covers only ENCOUNTER_EVENT_TYPES anyway. (5) Strip gap confirmed: route.ts:866-873 applies stripLeakedMechanics/canonicalizeToHitTarget only when _declEnc.active; the EVENT_LABEL regexes (combat-resolver.ts:380-381, 469-470, 520-521) list only the 6 encounter event names — never hp_change, inventory_add, xp_award, nor an 'apply_event:' prefix — so leaked non-combat event prose on a non-combat turn persists to the player verbatim (only a standalone JSON line containing id/delta/type/actorId would be caught, and only in active combat). (6) Selectability confirmed: local-services.ts:202-237 surfaces every installed Ollama base model (comment explicitly names gemma4) in the Settings dropdown; scripts/_set-campaign-model.ts and the 2026-06-04 commit series show the operator runs gemma4:12b live. No rulebook check needed — cited rule is model-config, not a data/rules.md rule. SEVERITY DOWNGRADE high→medium, two reasons: (a) the headline consequence — gemma never applies non-combat events and never reads the vault — is an explicitly documented, accepted trade-off (baked-models.ts:349-352: 'acceptable when the operator picks them for narration'), so it is intended behavior, not an unnoticed bug; (b) the current default path (qwen3 instruct) is completely unaffected (isWeakToolModel returns false). What remains genuinely broken on the operator-used gemma path is the prompt/directive-vs-dispatch contradiction (which partially defeats the very meltdown-avoidance offerTools:false was added for) and the unstripped non-combat event leakage — real player-visible degradation, but confined to a knowingly-degraded, non-default configuration. The recommendation (narration-only prompt variant when offerTools is false + extending strip helpers to all VAULT_EVENT_TYPES applied unconditionally) is sound and matches the code structure.

title: Vault path runs extractMemory on every turn but never consumes memory context — wasted per-turn local LLM call on the M4, digests/scene card unreachable by the vault prompt
evidence: route.ts:969-973 fires extractMemory(sessionId, provider, vaultMasterModel) after every persisted vault master message (an extra Ollama round-trip per turn on the production M4). loadMemoryContext is called ONLY on the baked path (route.ts:1044; the only non-test consumer per grep), and VaultPromptInput (prompt-builder.ts:17-90) has no chapterDigests/sceneCard/codexIndex fields — the vault prompt can never receive them. Vault long-horizon continuity rests solely on maybeCondense summarization (loop.ts:241-251). The memory artifacts are produced but structurally unconsumable on the path that is now the default for new campaigns.
recommendation: Either stop firing extractMemory on the vault path (saving one local LLM call per turn) or wire its output into the vault prompt/history. Decide explicitly — the current state pays the cost of the baked feature without its benefit.
verdict_notes: Confirmed all claims by direct reading. (1) route.ts:969-973: waitUntil(extractMemory(...)) fires after EVERY persisted vault master message; extractor.ts:262-330 shows no threshold short-circuit — light mode still makes a full completeMessage LLM call (input = full existing codex + last 2 messages, maxTokens 8192) on MEMORY_EXTRACTOR_MODEL ?? vaultMasterModel, i.e. a real per-turn Ollama round-trip on the prod M4 when the env var is unset (the local-default case per the extractor's own comment). (2) loadMemoryContext is called only at route.ts:1044, which is AFTER the vault branch's early return at route.ts:1013 — baked-path only; grep confirms no other non-test consumer. (3) VaultPromptInput (prompt-builder.ts:17-90) has no chapterDigests/sceneCard/codexIndex fields (exactly the MemoryContext shape from src/sessions/memory/context.ts:11-15), and src/ai/master/vault/ has zero references to memory context; the route comment "no scene card, no codex" shows the minimal prompt was deliberate while the extraction call was copy-pasted from the baked path (commit 6014209: "duplicated rather than extracted to a helper to minimize PR risk surface. Phase 02 may refactor" — never reconciled). (4) Vault continuity = MASTER_HISTORY_LIMIT(10) window + maybeCondense in loop.ts ~227-251, as claimed. (5) Vault IS the default for new campaigns (src/app/api/campaigns/route.ts:33). Compensating-code search came up empty: inventory enrichment (state/route.ts:130 → enrich-inventory.ts) only matches codex named_items by inventory slug, and inventory slugs for narrative items are created only by the baked-path add_narrative_item tool — so extractor output is effectively dead on vault campaigns; memory/status UI is informational only; no guard skips extraction on masterBackend==='vault'. Severity stays medium: fire-and-forget so it never blocks or corrupts the current turn, but it burns one local LLM generation per turn (input grows with codex size) on bandwidth-bound prod hardware, competing with the next turn's master inference, for artifacts the default path structurally cannot consume.

title: isSmallModel regex misclassifies the validated primary (and fallback/Max 3/14b) as a 3-4B model, capping every local master turn at num_predict=2048
evidence: local.ts:536 `const isSmallModel = /(?:llama3\.2.*3b|qwen3.*[34]b|gemma2?.*2b|dnd-master-(?:lite|balance))/i.test(input.model ?? '')`. The unanchored `qwen3.*[34]b` matches the '3b' inside the MoE suffix 'a3b' and the '4b' inside '14b' — verified by execution: qwen3:30b-a3b-instruct-2507-q4_K_M=true, qwen3:30b-a3b-instruct-2507=true, qwen3:30b-a3b=true, qwen3:14b=true (only bare qwen3:30b=false). Line 538 then sets `defaultMaxTokens = isSmallModel ? 2048 : (isThinkingOn ? 3000 : 4096)`, so the 3000 thinking tier and 4096 capable tier are unreachable for every qwen3 a3b model. The comment block at lines 520-533 states the empirical requirement this violates: for Max 3 'Raising from 1500→2500 reduced capping from 100% to 26% of turns. Raising further to 3000 covers heavy combat turns', and for qwen3-instruct 'keep 4096 — combat-heavy turns with long multi-tool narrations occasionally need the headroom'. Neither runVaultToolLoop nor runToolLoop passes input.maxTokens (grep: maxTokens only set by condense.ts:214=400 and extractor.ts:317=8192), so this default governs all master turns on both paths. Same alternative `gemma2?.*2b` also matches gemma4:12b (via '12b') but not gemma4:latest — inconsistent within the gemma family. The spike's own recommended num_predict for the primary was 2500 (model-selection.md line 57); the bug delivers 2048.
recommendation: Anchor the small-model patterns to the tag boundary, e.g. /qwen3:[34]b\b/ and /(?:^|[:/-])(?:llama3\.2:3b|gemma2?:2b)\b/, or replace the regex with an explicit slug set. Add a unit test asserting qwen3:30b-a3b-instruct-2507-q4_K_M, qwen3:30b-a3b and qwen3:14b are NOT small and that the a3b thinking family resolves to the 3000 tier.
verdict_notes: CONFIRMED. (1) Code verbatim at src/ai/provider/local.ts:536-538/547: unanchored qwen3.*[34]b verified by node execution to match 'a3b' and '14b' substrings — qwen3:30b-a3b-instruct-2507-q4_K_M, qwen3:30b-a3b, qwen3:14b all classify as small (only bare qwen3:30b escapes); gemma4:12b also matches via '12b' while gemma4:latest doesn't. (2) The misclassified model is the production primary on the current path: baked-models.ts:76 names qwen3:30b-a3b-instruct-2507-q4_K_M as REQ-030 primary selected as a base slug (Phase 03 retired baked tiers); spike doc model-selection.md lines 35/57 confirms primary + recommended num_predict 2500. Turn route line 700-705 passes userPrefs.aiMasterModel unmodified to runVaultToolLoop; vault/loop.ts:254 and tool-loop.ts:140 call completeMessage WITHOUT maxTokens (only condense.ts:214=400, extractor.ts:317=8192 set it, both off the master-turn path) — so every master turn gets num_predict=2048 instead of intended 4096/2500. (3) No compensating code: no tests reference isSmallModel/defaultMaxTokens; recent narration-only/empty-retry guards target weak-tool gemma, not the cap. (4) Git history (af2d0d9→5cedcb7→f235b55) strengthens the finding: the 1500→2500→3000 thinking tier was tuned while Max 3 ran as baked 'dnd-master-max3' (regex miss → tier applied); post-Phase-03 base-slug selection silently regresses that tuned tier to 2048 — unintentional regression, not a deliberate choice. SEVERITY downgraded high→medium per player impact on the CURRENT default path: the instruct primary is non-thinking (thinkingFlagFor=undefined, no CoT in the budget), so 2048 covers most turns; the code's own comment says the 4096 headroom is needed only 'occasionally' on combat-heavy multi-tool turns. Default-path harm = intermittent truncation of the longest combat narrations (plus the 2048-2500 band the spike config would cover). The severe mode (CoT capping → empty 'Master non ha prodotto risposta' turns, 26%+ cap rate extrapolated) only hits qwen3:30b-a3b base-slug selection (Max 3 family) — supported but not default. Recommended fix (anchor patterns to tag boundary + unit test asserting a3b/14b are NOT small) is correct.

title: Spike's battle-tested sampling parameters (repeat_penalty 1.13 'critical', temp/top_p/min_p) never applied at runtime — they live only in retired baked Modelfiles while the vault path runs un-baked base slugs
evidence: model-selection.md prescribes for the primary: temperature 0.7, top_p 0.9, min_p 0.05, repeat_penalty 1.13 ('critical for qwen3-a3b: prevents verbatim re-narration loop'), keep_alive 30m, and says 'These values are battle-tested in scripts/build-local-models.ts ... Bring them forward.' The runtime chat request (local.ts:547) sends only `options: { num_predict, num_ctx }` — no temperature/top_p/min_p/repeat_penalty anywhere in src/ai/provider/local.ts (grep confirms zero hits). The tuned PARAMETERs exist only as Modelfile overrides in build-local-models.ts:135-140 ('qwen3:30b-a3b-instruct-2507': { ... repeat_penalty: 1.13 }), but Phase 03 retired all qwen3 baked tiers (baked-models.ts:14-24: only dnd-master-plus/gpt-oss survives; 'the vault path runs them un-baked, REQ-030/031/032'). Net effect: the production primary runs on Ollama's stock sampling defaults, dropping the anti-re-narration penalty the spike marked critical. Also KEEP_ALIVE defaults to '5m' (local.ts:25) vs the spike's 30m, while the comment at local.ts:184-185 claims 'the KEEP_ALIVE window (default 60m)' — internally contradictory.
recommendation: Add the per-model tuned options (at minimum repeat_penalty 1.13, temperature 0.7, top_p 0.9, min_p 0.05 for the qwen3 a3b instruct family) to the options object in LocalProvider.completeMessage, keyed off the resolved base slug; align KEEP_ALIVE default (and its comment) with the spike's 30m.
verdict_notes: CONFIRMED, and slightly worse than reported. (1) src/ai/provider/local.ts:547 sends only { num_predict, num_ctx }; grep across src/ shows zero hits for temperature/top_p/min_p/repeat_penalty — the tuned values live only in scripts/build-local-models.ts:115-146 Modelfile PARAMETERs. (2) baked-models.ts:93-99 confirms Phase 03 retired all qwen3 baked tiers (only gpt-oss/dnd-master-plus survives); REQ-030 primary qwen3:30b-a3b-instruct-2507-q4_K_M runs un-baked on the vault path. (3) Independent check: `ollama show qwen3:30b-a3b-instruct-2507-q4_K_M` (the exact production slug) shows the library manifest ships repeat_penalty=1 (NO penalty — worse than the assumed ~1.1 stock), top_p 0.8, no min_p; only temperature 0.7 matches the spike. The spike's model-selection.md lines 55-63 verified verbatim ('repeat_penalty: 1.13 critical for qwen3-a3b: prevents verbatim re-narration loop ... Bring them forward'). (4) Compensating-control search FAILS on the default path: the prompt-level anti-repetition guard MASTER_ADVANCE_NARRATIVE_RULE (system-prompt.ts:1647, added after a real observed loop; build script cites session 7406a0a5) is injected only by buildMasterSystemPrompt (legacy path, route.ts:1168); the current default vault path uses buildVaultSystemPrompt (route.ts:258, vault/prompt-builder.ts) which contains no repetition guidance at all — so the vault primary has neither sampler-level nor prompt-level mitigation against a documented, previously observed failure mode. (5) KEEP_ALIVE sub-claim factually correct (local.ts:25 default '5m' vs comment at 184-186 claiming 'default 60m') but mitigated in practice: .env.local sets OLLAMA_KEEP_ALIVE=30m and setup-ollama-env.sh sets the daemon to 30m, so it's a minor hygiene issue. Severity stays medium: real player-facing degradation risk (stuck re-narration loop documented on this exact model family) on the current default path, but stochastic/intermittent rather than a guaranteed break, and temperature already matches via the library manifest. Recommendation in the finding is sound: key tuned options off the resolved base slug in LocalProvider.completeMessage (at minimum repeat_penalty 1.13 + min_p 0.05 for the qwen3 a3b family), and fix the 60m comment.

title: hit_dice_restore unclamped in Postgres leg and applicator, clamped to hit_dice_max in vault (and legacy seeds carry hit_dice_max=0)
evidence: The vault reducer clamps `min(hit_dice_max, remaining + count)` (projector.ts:522-526), but the PG leg computes `state.hitDiceRemaining + event.payload.count` with no upper bound (event-to-engine-mutation.ts:370) and the applicator's restore_hit_dice is likewise unbounded (applicator.ts:287). Postgres can exceed the character's hit-dice pool (pool max = character level, one die per level per rules.md line 109/§5.1; long rest recovers 'up to half maximum', rules.md line 341). Worse for parity: Phase-02 seeds omit hit_dice_max so the vault defaults it to 0 (projector.ts:203-204) — every hit_dice_restore is then clamped to 0 in the vault while PG increments freely, a guaranteed divergence in opposite directions.
rule_citation: data/rules.md §5.2 (line 341: 'Recover spent Hit Dice up to half maximum') + logical-consistency
recommendation: Clamp the PG leg and applicator to characters.hitDiceMax, and backfill hit_dice_max into existing vault seeds (or have the reducer fall back to level when hit_dice_max is 0).
verdict_notes: All four cited code locations verified verbatim: PG leg (event-to-engine-mutation.ts:367-373) and applicator (applicator.ts:283-289) add hit dice with no upper clamp; vault projector clamps to hit_dice_max (projector.ts:520-527); seed defaults hit_dice_max to 0 (projector.ts:203-204). Rule citation verified: data/rules.md §5.2 'Recover spent Hit Dice up to half maximum (minimum 1)'. Two legs of the finding are partially mitigated: (a) the applicator's only restore_hit_dice producer is engine longRest (src/engine/rests.ts:122-125) which pre-clamps recovered=min(used, floor(max/2)), so the engine path cannot overflow; (b) the unclamped PG leg only executes under dualWrite, which defaults to false (resolveDualWrite, src/lib/preferences.ts:174-177), so the opposite-direction divergence is latent, not active. However the seed gap is real, uncompensated, and live: the production seeder assembleCampaignSeedPayload (src/campaigns/seed-vault.ts:95-122) emits only id/name/hp_max/hp_current/spell_slots — no hit_dice_max — and no script backfills it. Confirmed in runtime data: every materialized character view across all 21 live campaigns (including active One Piece 3ef630db) shows hit_dice_max: 0 / hit_dice_remaining: 0, and zero hit_dice events have ever been emitted. On the current default path (sourceOfTruth='vault', initial-settings.ts:58) the client snapshot and the LLM's view both read vault hit dice (snapshot-reader.ts:203, projector.ts:1175), so the hit-dice mechanic is permanently dead (pool stuck at 0; restore clamps to min(0,...)). Bonus confirmation: parity-check.ts:170 hard-codes the vault side to 0 ('vault doesn't track' — stale), so hit-dice divergence detection is broken even with dualWrite on. Severity stays medium: no state corruption has occurred yet (no events emitted), healing remains possible via hp_change, but a core §5.1 short-rest mechanic is unusable on the default path for all campaigns. The fix recommendation is sound: seed hit_dice_max (level-based fallback in the reducer) and clamp the PG/applicator legs for defense-in-depth.

title: Attunement cap of 3 not enforced anywhere on the vault path
evidence: On the baked path the cap is enforced in the engine tool layer (MAX_ATTUNED = 3, engine/items.ts:63; handlers.ts:1419 returns 'attunement_cap_reached'). On the vault path: the validator only checks itemSlug length ≤ 64 (events-schema.ts:903-918), the vault reducer pushes unbounded (projector.ts:565-572), and the PG leg's own comment admits 'cap enforced engine-side, not here' (event-to-engine-mutation.ts:407-408) — but the engine handler never runs on vault campaigns. The LLM can attune any number of items.
recommendation: Enforce the cap in the attune reducer arm (no-op past 3, matching the engine's graceful-skip idiom) and mirror it in the PG leg, or reject the event at the dispatcher with a self-correcting error message.
verdict_notes: CONFIRMED at all four cited locations. (1) /src/ai/master/vault/projector.ts:565-573 — attune reducer arm: dedupe check, push, sort; no cap. (2) /src/sessions/event-to-engine-mutation.ts:405-425 — PG leg pushes unbounded; comment at 407-408 literally says 'cap enforced engine-side, not here'. (3) /src/engine/items.ts:63 — MAX_ATTUNED=3 exists but grep shows it is consulted ONLY in engine/tools/handlers.ts (line 1447, returns attunement_cap_reached); no vault-path usage. (4) /src/ai/master/vault/events-schema.ts:903-918 — validator checks only character non-empty + itemSlug length ≤64, stateless. Rule verified: data/rules.md line 523 (§10.1) 'max 3 attuned items per character'. Engine-handler-never-runs claim verified: dispatchVaultTool (src/ai/master/vault/tools.ts:296-396) routes attune through validateEvent (shape-only) then vault append + invokeEnginePathwayFromEvent (the direct-DB leg above); the engine tool handler is never invoked on vault campaigns. Searched for compensating controls and found NONE: vault prompt-builder.ts/turn-directive.ts/loop.ts contain zero attunement mentions (the baked system-prompt.ts cap guidance at lines 670-728 is NOT reused on the vault path, so qwen3 gets no instruction about the cap); the parity check cannot catch it because both legs push unbounded and agree; projector tests (tests/ai/master/vault/projector.test.ts:2048-2100) cover append/sort/idempotency only — no cap case, so the unbounded behavior is baked into the test suite. Aggravating evidence the auditor missed: the same vault reducer DOES enforce other PHB caps — death saves trigger stable at 3 (projector.ts:403) and exhaustion clamps at 6 with Math.min (projector.ts:485) — proving the attune arm's missing cap is an inconsistency, not deliberate design. Severity stays medium: real on the current default path (vault + qwen3, with no prompt guidance at all), but player impact is bounded — attunement events are rare, exceeding 3 requires a campaign granting 4+ attunement items, there is no crash/divergence/corruption (both legs stay consistent), and the fix matches an existing idiom. Recommendation in the finding is sound: enforce in the reducer arm (no-op at >= 3, like the exhaustion clamp) and mirror in the PG leg, importing MAX_ATTUNED per items.ts's 'do not hardcode 3 elsewhere' directive.

title: One corrupt events.md line silently rolls the player back to stale Postgres state (vault campaigns)
evidence: parseEventsFile is fail-fast: any unparseable line throws (projector.ts:995-1000). buildClientSnapshot catches ALL vault errors with only a console.warn and falls back to reading session_state from Postgres (client-snapshot.ts:157-179). For a vault-native campaign (sourceOfTruth='vault', dualWrite=false — the default per initial-settings.ts), Postgres session_state was last written at session creation, so the player's UI silently shows pre-campaign HP/conditions with no surfaced error, while the turn route's unprotected replay calls (route.ts:361, 561) throw. The corruption is invisible to the player and the operator unless they read server logs.
rule_citation: logical-consistency (mission item e — what the player sees on failure)
recommendation: Distinguish corruption from absence: on parse failure, surface an operator-visible error state in the snapshot (statusFlag) instead of silently serving stale Postgres state, and add a vault:fsck path to the runbook.
verdict_notes: CONFIRMED core claim, with one sub-claim corrected. Verified: (1) parseEventsFile throws on any unparseable line (projector.ts:990-1001, intentional spike-008 fail-fast); (2) materializeFromVault (snapshot-reader.ts:106-132) returns null only for ENOENT/empty/unseeded — parse throws propagate; (3) buildClientSnapshot (client-snapshot.ts:144-179) catches ALL vault errors with console.warn only and falls back to Postgres session_state, also losing vaultEncounter so the combat tracker silently empties (combat_actors is never written for vault campaigns); (4) defaults confirmed: initial-settings.ts:46-59 sets sourceOfTruth='vault', DEFAULT_PREFERENCES.dualWrite=false, and Postgres session_state mutations only run when ctx.dualWrite===true (vault/tools.ts:340), so the fallback row holds creation-time values (forge.ts:97-101 seeds hpCurrent=hpMax, no conditions) — the player silently sees pre-campaign full HP; (5) no compensating code: statusFlag hard-coded null (snapshot-reader.ts:242), no fsck tooling, no corrupt-line test found. REFUTED sub-claim: route.ts:361 and :561 are NOT unprotected — every replay call in the turn route (361, 370, 453, 484, 561, 868, 916) is wrapped in try/catch with warn+fallthrough. This correction makes the failure MORE silent than reported: on a corrupt line nothing errors to the player anywhere — combat resolution, the encounter opener, monster turns and apply_event view regeneration all degrade to warn-and-continue while the UI serves stale state. Severity stays medium: trigger requires actual file corruption (appends are single O_APPEND writes, so mostly crash-mid-write or operator hand-edit — the latter explicitly contemplated by the projector's operator-text-editor docs), and recovery is fixing one line; but when it fires the dual failure (stale UI + dead mechanics) is invisible to both player and operator except in server logs. The recommendation (distinguish corruption from absence, surface an error state) is sound.

title: build-local-models.ts executes main() on import, causing an undocumented unhandled rejection (process.exit(1)) during the test run
evidence: scripts/build-local-models.ts:426 has a top-level `main().catch((e) => { console.error('[build-local-models] fatal:', e); process.exit(1); });` with no entry-point guard (no `require.main === module` / `import.meta.main` check anywhere in the 429-line file). tests/scripts/build-local-models.slim.test.ts:57 does `import { buildStaticSystemContent } from '../../scripts/build-local-models';`, which runs the whole CLI as an import side effect; under the test's mocked DB env it fails and hits process.exit(1) at line 428. Vitest run output: 'Vitest caught 1 unhandled error during the test run. ... Unhandled Rejection: Error: process.exit unexpectedly called with "1" ... scripts/build-local-models.ts:428:11 ... This error originated in "tests/scripts/build-local-models.slim.test.ts"' and vitest warns 'This might cause false positive tests.' This unhandled error is NOT in the documented baseline (.planning/phases/09-v2-monster-turns/deferred-items.md contains no mention of build-local-models), and it contributes to the suite's non-zero exit even though the test file itself passes.
recommendation: Guard the CLI entry point so main() only runs when the script is executed directly (e.g. wrap the `main().catch(...)` call at scripts/build-local-models.ts:426 in an entry-point check, or move main() invocation to a separate bin wrapper file), so importing buildStaticSystemContent from tests has no side effects.
verdict_notes: empirical (direct run)

title: end_turn with missing/non-string response wipes all previously accumulated narration (empty-string overwrite, ?? instead of fallback)
evidence: tools.ts:273: 'const response = typeof raw.response === "string" ? raw.response : "";' — a model calling end_turn{} (no args, a common weak-model behavior) yields endTurnResponse: ''. loop.ts:350: 'finalText = result.endTurnResponse ?? finalText;' — '' is not nullish, so the empty string OVERWRITES every text block accumulated (and already streamed to the client as message-chunk SSE) in earlier iterations. The route then sees empty finalText: on a non-combat turn it triggers the retry (compounding the double-apply finding above); on a server-resolved combat turn it skips persistence and emits only a silent state refresh (route.ts:993-999) — the player saw streamed narration that is never persisted and vanishes on reload.
recommendation: Change loop.ts:350 to prefer a non-empty response: finalText = result.endTurnResponse?.trim() ? result.endTurnResponse : finalText. Add a unit test for end_turn with {} input after accumulated text.
verdict_notes: Code mechanics CONFIRMED: tools.ts:273 coerces missing/non-string end_turn response to '' (behavior pinned by tests/ai/master/vault/tools.test.ts:181-190), and loop.ts:350 'finalText = result.endTurnResponse ?? finalText' is a dead fallback since endTurnResponse is always a string for end_turn — '' overwrites all text accumulated at loop.ts:276 (already streamed as narrative_delta SSE). No adapter-level schema enforcement (ollama-adapter.ts:211 passes arguments verbatim; required:['response'] is advisory). Route behavior also confirmed: empty finalText → full-loop retry at route.ts:792-795 (first-run apply_event mutations already persisted, so retry risks double-apply) and skipped persistence at route.ts:964. HOWEVER the finding's worst impact claim is REFUTED by a compensating guard: every server-resolved combat turn (resolverFired/monsterLoopRan/openerRan/encounterActive/combatDeclaration/rollResult) is forced narration-only via isNarrationOnlyTurn (combat-resolver.ts:98-116) → offerTools:false (route.ts:724-734) → offeredTools=[] (loop.ts:164), so end_turn cannot be called on those turns and the 'combat narration vanishes via silent state refresh (route.ts:993-999)' scenario cannot fire through this bug. Begin turns and weak-tool (gemma) models are likewise tool-less everywhere. Remaining exposure: non-combat tools-offered turns on qwen3-a3b-instruct, which is spike-validated for 100% tool compliance — end_turn{} is possible but not observed for the default model. If it fires, the player usually recovers via the retry (at the cost of possible double-applied non-combat mutations — overlap with the separate double-apply finding); only a double failure loses narration with a retry toast. Bug is real and the recommended one-line fix (prefer non-empty response, plus a unit test for end_turn{} after accumulated text) is correct, but severity downgraded medium→low: the catastrophic combat variant is structurally impossible on the current default path, and the live variant is low-frequency with a partial recovery path.

title: Vault system prompt self-contradiction: campaign directory declared 'reserved — populated in a later release' while the same prompt instructs reading combat.md and character frontmatter from it
evidence: prompt-builder.ts:333 emits 'Active campaign: /campaigns/<id>/ (reserved — populated in a later release)' on every vault prompt. The Combat lifecycle block in the SAME prompt says 'The live combat tracker is at campaigns/<campaignId>/combat.md — updated automatically' (prompt-builder.ts:227-228), the turn directive says 'confronta il totale con la CA del bersaglio (vedi combat.md)' (turn-directive.ts:158), and the dispatcher error message tells the model to 'Read the characters/<slug>.md materialized view frontmatter' (tools.ts:315). A model that believes the 'reserved' line will skip reading the live state it is elsewhere ordered to consult.
recommendation: Update the Knowledge-layout line to describe the real campaign layout (events.md is server-owned; combat.md and characters/<slug>.md are readable views). Re-lock the REQ-022 snapshot hash after the wording change.
verdict_notes: CONFIRMED as a real self-contradiction, but severity downgraded medium->low. Verified verbatim: src/ai/master/vault/prompt-builder.ts:333 unconditionally emits 'Active campaign: /campaigns/<id>/ (reserved — populated in a later release)' while the SAME prompt (vaultMutations=true, the live-campaign config) emits the combat lifecycle block at lines 227-228 pointing to campaigns/<campaignId>/combat.md; turn-directive.ts:158/244 and tools.ts:315 also order the model to consult campaign files; data/vault/tools/apply_event.md:24,28 documents those paths as readable; tools.ts:233-262 (Decision 4 root routing) proves /campaigns/... reads actually work. The line dates to commit f4f0fd7 (Phase 02) and was never updated. Severity downgrade rationale (player impact on current default path): (1) character UUIDs are injected directly into the prompt roster, so the tools.ts:315 read instruction is an error-recovery path only; (2) Phase 08-10 server-authoritative resolution handles to-hit vs AC, monster turns, and encounter opening with injected narration directives — the 'vedi combat.md' directive fires only on the !serverResolved fallthrough; (3) monster names usually persist in the 10-message history; (4) the stale line discourages but never blocks reads, and the in-prompt combat block plus apply_event.md (which the model reads first) both contradict it with more specific, later instructions. Residual exposure is limited to fallback branches (resolver fallthrough, numbered-monster exact-name matching), where a literal-minded weak model skipping the combat.md read degrades to the existing LLM-fallback target matcher. Fix is a one-line prompt wording change; note the recommendation is correct that the locked hash in tests/ai/master/vault/prompt-builder.test.ts (60e56767...) must be re-locked.

title: Tonal frame / engagement profile feature silently absent on the vault path (baked-only), answering mission item (d): no mechanism for initial-state mutations on the vault opener
evidence: Begin-turn flow on vault: offerTools:false + tonalMandate:false means the opener is pure narration — by design nothing can be read or mutated (initial campaign state arrives via external seeding, e.g. vault:flip / campaign_initialized, not the turn loop). Consequence: set_tonal_frame exists only as a baked meta-tool (route.ts:1272 requiredToolsBeforeEnd; snap.state.tonalFrame consumed only by buildMasterSystemPrompt at route.ts:1199), and VaultPromptInput has no tonalFrame field — a vault campaign can never set or use a tonal frame, while begin-message.ts:9-16 documents this as 'the vault path never reads tonalFrame'. This is a coherent (non-buggy) state today, but it is an undocumented feature loss for campaigns flipped from baked (their stored tonalFrame is silently ignored) and there is no replacement mechanism.
recommendation: Document the divergence (vault = no tonal frame) or add tonalFrame as a VaultPromptInput passed from the campaign row; if the feature is dropped, remove the now-dead tonal UI/state for vault campaigns.
verdict_notes: Verified all cited locations. (1) route.ts:1199-1200 passes snap.state.tonalFrame/engagementProfile to buildMasterSystemPrompt only on the baked branch; the vault branch (lines 236-1011) early-returns before it. (2) route.ts:1272 requiredToolsBeforeEnd:['set_tonal_frame'] is baked-only; set_tonal_frame exists solely in the engine tool surface (handlers.ts:673, tools/index.ts:753, meta-tools.ts:104), unreachable from the vault loop. (3) VaultPromptInput (vault/prompt-builder.ts:17-90) has no tonalFrame field; grep across src/ai/master/vault/ finds zero tonalFrame/engagementProfile references — no compensating code. (4) Vault begin passes tonalMandate:false (route.ts:285) and offerTools:false (route.ts:734; loop.ts:164 empties the tool list), so the opener is narration-only as claimed; begin-message.ts:9-16 explicitly states the vault path never reads tonalFrame. (5) campaigns.tonal_frame column exists (campaigns.ts:143), snapshot.ts:501 still hydrates it, and page.tsx:148 still ships it to the client — so a baked→vault flipped campaign's stored frame is silently ignored and the client state is dead weight on vault. Rule citation is 'logical-consistency', not a D&D rule, so data/rules.md is N/A. One correction to the finding's framing: the divergence IS documented in code comments (begin-message.ts module doc, route.ts:46-47/281-284) — it is undocumented only at the user/project-docs level. The state is deliberate and coherent (the finding concedes this); impact on the current default vault+qwen3 path is a subtle loss of tone-guidance prompt blocks, with tone still carried by the verbatim premise. Confirmed as a real feature-parity/documentation gap at severity low.

title: Read-only vault campaigns: prompt advertises 3 tools but the loop always offers 4 and /tools/index.md documents apply_event — acknowledged belt-and-suspenders, minor model confusion risk
evidence: With vaultMutations=false the prompt says 'use any of the 3 listed tools' (route.ts:261 toolCount:3) but loop.ts:164 always passes all 4 VAULT_TOOL_DEFINITIONS (apply_event included) when offerTools !== false, and the prompt's mandated first read, /tools/index.md, lists apply_event with full combat semantics (index.md:8). A read-only model is thus invited to call apply_event, which fails with the dispatcher's campaignId isError (tools.ts:282-287) and burns loop iterations against the 20-call cap. The route comment (route.ts:225-231) documents this as intentional; listed for completeness as a prompt-vs-dispatcher mismatch (mission item e).
recommendation: Filter apply_event out of offeredTools when campaignId is not forwarded (the dispatcher guard stays as defense-in-depth), or split /tools/index.md into read-only and read-write variants.
verdict_notes: Every citation verified: route.ts:261 sets toolCount 3 when vaultMutations=false; prompt-builder.ts:337-338 mandates reading /tools/index.md first then says 'use any of the 3 listed tools'; loop.ts:164 unconditionally offers all 4 VAULT_TOOL_DEFINITIONS (apply_event included, tools.ts:46-108) whenever offerTools!==false, with no filtering by the mutations gate anywhere; data/vault/tools/index.md:8 documents apply_event with combat semantics in the single shared index (no read-only variant); tools.ts:282-287 returns a clean isError when ctx.campaignId is absent, and route.ts:739 forwards campaignId only when vaultMutationsEnabled. The route comment (route.ts:226-231) explicitly documents this as intentional belt-and-suspenders. So the prompt-vs-tool-surface mismatch is real but acknowledged and defended. Severity stays low (not lower-able in schema, and low is right): the mismatch only manifests in Phase 01 read-only mode (vaultMutations=false); the current production/default path runs read-write (combat via apply_event to events.md is the actively shipped path), where toolCount=4 and everything agrees. Worst-case player impact in read-only mode is the model wasting a few of the 20 loop iterations on a clean explanatory error before recovering. The recommendation (filter apply_event from offeredTools when campaignId is not forwarded, keeping the dispatcher guard) is sound and cheap, but this is a polish item, not a player-facing defect on the default path.

title: No allowlist restricting campaigns to spike-validated models — any Ollama model (gemma4, eliminated qwen3:30b-a3b base) is selectable through UI, API, and scratch script
evidence: Per-campaign model is stored in campaigns.settings.aiMasterModel (JSONB) and validated only by validateSettingsPatch (preferences.ts:626-637): when the resolved provider is 'local', the ONLY check is `typeof m === 'string' && m.length > 0 && m.length <= 200` — isKnownMasterModel (ai-models.ts:159, cloud catalogs only) is skipped for local. The intended whitelist exists but is dead code: LOCAL_LLM_PATTERNS / matchesLlmWhitelist (local-services.ts:99-108, 'phase 1 supports qwen3 and gpt-oss families') has zero callers (grep across src/ finds only the definition). fetchOllamaModels (local-services.ts:229-236) deliberately surfaces every installed non-embedding model — its own comment lists '(b) Raw generative base models (qwen3, gemma4, mistral, ...)'. scripts/_set-campaign-model.ts writes any slug straight into campaigns.settings with no validation at all and is self-described as the 'THROWAWAY experiment helper (gemma4 comparison, plan 2026-05-28)' — the documented vector by which never-validated gemma4 entered a live campaign, triggering the 2026-06-04+ hotfix chain (commits 769029c..2aea307). The spike doc explicitly forbids this ('✗ Adding new candidate models without re-running the sweep'; 'qwen3:30b-a3b BASE — eliminated permanently', 'llama3.2:3b — 0% tool compliance'), yet both eliminated models remain selectable; baked-models.ts:326 even keeps an active support tier for the eliminated qwen3:30b-a3b base (thinkingFlagFor → true, dedicated MASTER_BRIEF_THINKING_RULE in system-prompt.ts:1663).
recommendation: Wire matchesLlmWhitelist (or a stricter validated-pool list: primary q4_K_M, instruct fallback, gpt-oss:20b regression baseline) into both fetchOllamaModels and the local branch of validateSettingsPatch's aiMasterModel check; surface non-validated models, if at all, behind an explicit experimental flag with a UI warning. Delete or guard scripts/_set-campaign-model.ts.
verdict_notes: All facts confirmed by independent reading: (1) preferences.ts:626-637 local branch validates aiMasterModel only as a non-empty string <=200 chars, skipping isKnownMasterModel (cloud-only catalog at ai-models.ts:159); (2) matchesLlmWhitelist (local-services.ts:106) has zero production callers — only its own test file; git history shows it was wired into fetchOllamaModels at 483d499, removed at 8ee94f5, and NOT restored by 421832d which re-exposed raw models; (3) fetchOllamaModels (local-services.ts:229-236) deliberately surfaces every non-embedding Ollama model, commit 421832d verified gemma4:latest and eliminated qwen3:30b-a3b base appear in the dropdown; (4) scripts/_set-campaign-model.ts writes any slug into campaigns.settings with no validation; (5) spike doc model-selection.md says exactly what's cited (base model eliminated permanently, llama3.2 0% compliance, no new models without re-running the sweep); (6) baked-models.ts:326 keeps thinkingFlagFor=true for the eliminated base. HOWEVER severity is overstated: raw-model exposure was a deliberate product decision (421832d, to fix an empty dropdown breaking REQ-031/032/034 and to enable the operator's gemma4 comparison); the 2026-06-04 hotfix chain added layered runtime guards that neutralize the known-bad families (isWeakToolModel forces gemma narration-only on every turn with server-owned combat, local.ts streaming CoT filter strips markerless/<think> leak, empty-narration retry, scatto-guard; thinkingFlagFor=true + envelope stripping is itself a mitigation for the base model's CoT leak); llama3.2:3b carries a UI warning. On the CURRENT default path (vault backend, validated qwen3 instruct primary) player impact is zero — the gap only manifests when the single operator explicitly selects a never-validated model via Settings or deliberately runs the throwaway script. Residual risk is defense-in-depth for FUTURE unvalidated pulls (no guard, no warning) — real but low severity per the player-impact rubric. The recommendation to wire matchesLlmWhitelist conflicts with the operator's demonstrated intent to keep gemma4 selectable for experiments; an experimental-flag/warning approach fits better than a hard allowlist.

title: OLLAMA_NUM_CTX default 65536 is infeasible on the 32GB M4 production target by the code's own KV-cost estimate; spike specified 16384
evidence: local.ts:34 `const NUM_CTX = envPositiveInt('OLLAMA_NUM_CTX', 65536)`, applied to every local request (master turns, wizard, language detect). The adjacent comment (lines 32-33) states 'each extra 8k of num_ctx allocates ~2-4GB of KV cache RAM' — by that estimate 65536 ctx allocates ~16-32GB of KV, which cannot coexist with the ~18GB q4_K_M primary on the 32GB production Mac Mini M4 (model-selection.md Constraints: 'M4 RAM (32GB): primary q4_K_M model = ~18GB ... ~28GB used'). The spike's validated config is num_ctx 16384 ('7K baked + 3K dynamic + 5K history + cushion'). The vault path's system prompt is ~6k tokens (initial-settings.ts:12-14), so 64k is also 4x oversized for the actual prompt budget; the legacy baked path even enforces MASTER_PROMPT_BUDGET=12500 (route.ts:1109) specifically because qwen3:30b-A3B 'returns empty content when total input tokens exceed ~13K' (route.ts:1076-1077) — making a 64k allocation pure RAM cost with no usable benefit on the validated models.
recommendation: Lower the default to the spike-validated 16384 (or derive per masterBackend: ~16k vault / larger only for the gpt-oss regression baseline), keeping OLLAMA_NUM_CTX as the override. Verify on the M4 that the default does not force macOS swap/Metal eviction during prefill.
verdict_notes: Verified all cited code: local.ts:34 defaults OLLAMA_NUM_CTX to 65536 and sends it as options.num_ctx on every local request (completeMessage:547, detectLanguage:581, proposeWizard:616), shared by vault and baked backends via the LocalProvider singleton. The repo's own evidence condemns the value: build-local-models.ts:100-105 states 16384 keeps KV ~3GB vs ~13GB at 65536 on a 24B dense model, 'preventing memory pressure and swap' on 32GB Macs; spike model-selection.md:58 validates num_ctx 16384 and its Constraints (line 117) confirm ~18GB q4_K_M primary with ~28GB total used on the 32GB M4. initial-settings.ts:12-14 (vault ~6k prompt) and route.ts (MASTER_PROMPT_BUDGET=12500, qwen3:30b-A3B ~13K empty-content cliff) match the finding's citations exactly. The baked Modelfile num_ctx 16384 does NOT compensate, since request options override Modelfile params in Ollama. One math correction: for the actual MoE primary (qwen3:30b-a3b, 48 layers, 4 KV heads x 128 dim) f16 KV at 65536 is ~6GB, not 16-32GB — but 18GB model + ~6GB KV + Node/Postgres/macOS still exceeds 32GB, so the infeasibility claim survives. HOWEVER, severity downgraded from medium to low because the default is not exercised on the current production path: .env.local sets OLLAMA_NUM_CTX=16384 and Next.js loads .env.local in production (next start) too, and scripts/setup-ollama-env.sh (README 'Critical for unified-memory Macs', run from ~/.zshrc) sets launchctl setenv OLLAMA_NUM_CTX=16384 as a second layer. The unsafe default only fires in a fresh/misconfigured environment — a realistic gap since .env.example documents zero OLLAMA vars — making this a latent default-safety/template footgun with no player impact on the currently configured vault+qwen3 path. The recommendation (lower the default to 16384, keep env override) is sound hygiene; adding OLLAMA_NUM_CTX to .env.example would close the fresh-setup hole.

title: Brand-new local campaign with no inherited model resolves aiMasterModel to empty string — request ships model:'' to Ollama; OLLAMA_MASTER_MODEL env is ignored as a default
evidence: Campaign creation snapshots the host's RAW prefs (forge.ts:52-63 → initialCampaignSettings); if the host never picked a local model, the campaign settings carry no aiMasterModel. getCampaignSettings (preferences.ts:450-451) resolves `prefs.aiMasterModel ?? envDefaultMasterModel(provider)`, and envDefaultMasterModel returns the empty string for provider 'local' (preferences.ts:90-91 'caller must override with runtime list' — but no runtime caller does). The turn route forwards it unchecked: route.ts:700 `const vaultMasterModel = userPrefs.aiMasterModel` → loop.ts:258 `...(model !== undefined && { model })` ('' !== undefined, so model:'' is sent) → local.ts chat body → Ollama 400 (model required/not found) → failed turn. There is an OLLAMA_MASTER_MODEL env var in the codebase, but it is read ONLY by detectLanguage (local.ts:571) — never as the master-turn default, so a MASTER_PROVIDER=local deployment has no env-level way to set the default master model.
recommendation: In envDefaultMasterModel, return process.env.OLLAMA_MASTER_MODEL ?? '<validated primary slug>' for provider 'local' (mirroring the openai/gemini/anthropic branches), or fail fast in the turn route with a clear 'no local model configured' error instead of shipping an empty model to Ollama.
verdict_notes: Mechanics CONFIRMED at every cited location: envDefaultMasterModel('local') returns '' (preferences.ts:90-91; the quoted comment is actually in ai-models.ts:134 but behavior matches); getCampaignSettings resolves prefs.aiMasterModel ?? '' for local (preferences.ts:450-451); route.ts:700 forwards it with no empty-model guard; loop.ts:258 ('' !== undefined) ships model:'' ; local.ts:541 puts it in the Ollama body and chat() throws 'ollama chat 400' (route.ts:1381 comment shows the team already met '400: model is required' in the memory extractor); OLLAMA_MASTER_MODEL is read ONLY at local.ts:571 (detectLanguage) — never as the master-turn default. HOWEVER, reachability on the current default path is blocked by three compensating layers the finding missed: (1) the user-prefs API (src/app/api/preferences/route.ts) allows only ttsAutoplay, so the host prefs forge.ts snapshots cannot normally carry aiProvider:'local' — a brand-new campaign resolves provider from MASTER_PROVIDER, which is unset in .env.local/.env.example/.env.production.local, defaulting to anthropic+claude-sonnet-4-5; (2) the campaign settings UI (settings-client.tsx:204-208) always pairs local with the first runtime Ollama model, and validateSettingsPatch rejects empty-string models (preferences.ts:629), so the UI cannot persist local-without-model; (3) only a raw API PATCH {aiProvider:'local'} without a model, or an ops decision to set MASTER_PROVIDER=local (supported at preferences.ts:86 but used nowhere in repo envs), produces the broken state. The missing env default and missing fail-fast are real defects worth fixing (recommendation is sound), but no player on the current vault/qwen3 default path can hit it — downgraded medium → low.

title: Weak-tool-model gate (gemma) exists only on the vault path; legacy baked path hands gemma the full tool surface, and gemma never receives the anti-CoT nudge
evidence: isWeakToolModel (baked-models.ts:354-359, matches /\bgemma/i; correctly excludes the qwen3 instruct primary per its own comment 'NOT weak: qwen3-a3b-instruct (spike-validated 100% tool compliance)') is consulted at exactly ONE call site: the vault path's offerTools gate (route.ts:724). The legacy/baked path (route.ts:1256+, runToolLoop with the full meta-tool surface) performs no weak-model check, and resolveMasterBackend defaults to 'baked' for any pre-migration campaign with masterBackend unset (preferences.ts:122-130), so selecting gemma on such a campaign reproduces every meltdown mode the vault hotfixes patched (CoT leak on tools, fake combat) with zero guard. Separately, the strongest available anti-CoT mitigation — the '[NO REASONING IN OUTPUT …]' user-message nudge — is gated on isThinkingModel (local.ts:466), which is `thinkingFlagFor(model) !== undefined` (local.ts:42-44); thinkingFlagFor returns undefined for gemma, so the one model family documented as the markerless-CoT leaker (route.ts:353, 721; combat-resolver.ts:81) is the only local family that never gets the nudge.
recommendation: Either apply isWeakToolModel on the baked path too (force narration-only or reject the model), or — preferably, combined with the allowlist finding — block gemma-family selection outright. If gemma stays selectable, extend the noReasoning nudge condition to `isThinkingModel(model) || isWeakToolModel(model)`.
verdict_notes: All factual claims verified by independent reading. (1) isWeakToolModel (baked-models.ts:354-359, /\bgemma/i) has exactly one call site: the vault branch offerTools gate at route.ts:724 — confirmed by grep across src/. (2) The legacy baked branch (route.ts:1258 runToolLoop with buildToolDefinitions meta-tool surface) has no weak-model check; tool-loop.ts and the meta-dispatcher contain no gemma/weak guard. (3) resolveMasterBackend (preferences.ts:122-130) defaults to 'baked' when stored value and MASTER_BACKEND env are unset, so pre-cutover campaigns land on the unguarded path. (4) Gemma is freely selectable: preferences.ts:626-637 skips isKnownMasterModel validation for the local provider (any string ≤200 chars), and fetchOllamaModels (local-services.ts:229-236) deliberately surfaces raw bases incl. gemma in the Settings dropdown. (5) Nudge gap confirmed: local.ts:466 gates the noReasoning preamble on isThinkingModel = thinkingFlagFor !== undefined (local.ts:42-44); thinkingFlagFor (baked-models.ts:318-335) matches only qwen3/deepseek-r1/gpt-oss and returns undefined for gemma, so the one documented markerless-CoT leaker never gets the nudge. (6) Searched for compensating controls: reasoning-strip/stripThinkingFromContent only handles <think>-tagged CoT (gemma's leak is markerless per the code's own comments); no guard in runToolLoop, no UI warning, no allowlist. Severity downgraded medium→low per rubric: the CURRENT default path (vault backend + qwen3 instruct) is fully guarded by the route.ts:724 gate; the meltdown reproduces only on a legacy baked-backend campaign with an operator-selected gemma model — a never-validated combination with no player impact on the default path, though it is a genuine latent trap since the dropdown offers gemma with zero friction. Recommendation in the finding (apply isWeakToolModel on the baked path or block gemma selection; extend the nudge condition) is sound.

title: Dead model whitelist plus stale Settings empty-state copy claiming a qwen3/gpt-oss filter that no longer exists
evidence: LOCAL_LLM_PATTERNS / matchesLlmWhitelist (local-services.ts:96-108) is exported but has no callers anywhere in src/ — fetchOllamaModels filters only embedders (lines 229-236). Meanwhile the Settings dropdown's empty state (settings-client.tsx:337) still reads 'No qwen3/gpt-oss installed in Ollama', describing the whitelist behavior that was removed — misleading the operator about what the dropdown will show (it shows gemma4, mistral, any non-embedding model).
recommendation: Either re-wire matchesLlmWhitelist into fetchOllamaModels (preferred, see the allowlist finding) or delete it and update the empty-state copy to 'No models installed in Ollama'.
verdict_notes: Confirmed both halves. (1) matchesLlmWhitelist/LOCAL_LLM_PATTERNS (src/lib/local-services.ts:99-108) has no production callers — repo-wide grep finds only its own unit test (tests/lib/local-services-whitelist.test.ts) and old plan docs. Git history: 483d499 wired the whitelist into fetchOllamaModels, 8ee94f5 removed it, and the current fetchOllamaModels (lines 229-236) filters only embedders (bert family / 'embed' name) plus TIER_LABELS gating for baked models — gemma/mistral/any generative model passes. (2) settings-client.tsx:337 still shows 'No qwen3/gpt-oss installed in Ollama' on empty dropdown; the data chain (fetchLocalServicesStatus → buildAiStatus → fetchOllamaModels → localServices.ai.models → availableModels) has no compensating whitelist anywhere, so the copy describes removed behavior and misleads the operator (any non-embedding model would actually populate the list). Extra corroboration: stale doc comment at local-services.ts:163-165 still claims 'filters by the LLM whitelist'. No D&D rule cited, so rules.md not implicated. Severity stays low: on the current default path (qwen3 installed) the empty state never renders and the dead export is inert — purely operator-facing copy in a rare edge case plus dead code. The test file keeping the dead function 'green' is the only thing masking it from a dead-code sweep.

title: Death-save semantics diverge across the three implementations (PG dual-write leg vs vault reducer vs applicator)
