evidence: Three asymmetries: (1) death_save_fail at 3 failures — vault reducer stores `{successes:0, failures:3}` 'for traceability' (projector.ts:428) matching the applicator (applicator.ts:605), but the PG dual-write leg resets to `{successes:0, failures:0}` (event-to-engine-mutation.ts:258) → guaranteed parity divergence on every PC death. (2) death_save_success at 3 — vault reducer and applicator both push 'unconscious' into conditions (projector.ts:410-413, applicator.ts:575-583); the PG leg only sets deathSaves+stable, never touching conditions (event-to-engine-mutation.ts:243-247). (3) death_save_recover_at_one — vault removes 'unconscious' and clears both stable AND dead flags (projector.ts:452-455); the PG leg sets hpCurrent=1 and clears only `stable`, leaving 'unconscious' in conditions (event-to-engine-mutation.ts:275-279), so a Postgres-sourced UI keeps showing the PC unconscious after a nat-20 recovery.
recommendation: Make event-to-engine-mutation.ts mirror the vault reducer byte-for-byte for all four death_save_* events: preserve failures:3 on death, add 'unconscious' on 3-success stabilization, and remove 'unconscious' + clear dead on recover_at_one. Add a parity test that replays each death-save sequence through both legs and diffs the normalized states.
verdict_notes: Verified all three asymmetries by reading the cited code: (1) event-to-engine-mutation.ts:256-260 resets deathSaves to {0,0} on death vs {0,3} in projector.ts:424-430 and applicator.ts:601-608; (2) PG leg lines 243-247 never touches conditions on 3-success stabilization while projector.ts:403-414 and applicator.ts:573-591 ensure 'unconscious'; (3) PG leg lines 272-279 leaves 'unconscious' and the dead flag intact on recover_at_one while projector.ts:446-456 removes/clears both. data/rules.md §3.18 confirms nat 20 = 'regain 1 HP, become conscious', so the PG leg violates the rulebook, and the module's own header claims to mirror the applicator (it doesn't). No test file pins the PG-leg behavior, so it's a bug, not a decision. HOWEVER, severity 'high' is refuted on player impact: (a) queried the live Supabase DB — ZERO campaigns have settings.dualWrite=true (resolveDualWrite has no env override), so the dual-write branch at vault/tools.ts:340 is never taken and event-to-engine-mutation.ts is dead code on every current campaign including One Piece (masterBackend=vault, vaultMutations=true, dualWrite unset, sourceOfTruth=vault); (b) all live vault campaigns have sourceOfTruth=vault, so client-snapshot.ts reads vault-materialized state — the claimed 'Postgres-sourced UI keeps showing unconscious' requires the 03-A soak config (sourceOfTruth=postgres + dualWrite=true) that no campaign has; (c) the 'guaranteed parity divergence on every PC death' mechanism is wrong: parity-check.ts:166-167 hardcodes the vault side to death_saves {0,0} and flags all-false (stale 'vault doesn't track yet' comments), so the deathSaves mismatch is invisible to the parity checker, and the flags divergence that does fire is caused by the stale normalizer regardless of this asymmetry; only the conditions asymmetry would be genuinely flagged. Net: real divergence worth fixing before the operator's planned dual-write soak (it would pollute the parity audit the soak exists to run), but zero player impact on the current default path. Downgraded high -> low.

title: parity-check hardcodes vault-side defaults for fields the vault now tracks: guaranteed false positives and masked real divergences
evidence: normalizeVaultState ignores the real CharacterState values and hardcodes `temp_hp: 0, death_saves: {0,0}, flags: all-false, concentrating_on: null, exhaustion_level: 0, hit_dice_remaining: 0, attunements: [], resources_used: {}, xp: 0, level: 1` with comments 'vault doesn't track' (parity-check.ts:162-174) — but CharacterState HAS tracked all of these since Phase 03 (projector.ts:139-150) and the seed populates them. The Postgres side uses LIVE values (`pgChar.level ?? 1`, `pgState.hitDiceRemaining ?? 0`, `[...pgChar.attunedItems].sort()`, parity-check.ts:221-229). Consequence: any character with level>1, xp>0, hitDiceRemaining>0 (set non-null at session creation), or attuned items produces a recorded divergence on EVERY apply_event (audit-table spam, `divergence` noise returned to the LLM), while a genuine vault-vs-PG mismatch in temp_hp/death_saves/exhaustion etc. where PG sits at default is reported as a match (false negative).
rule_citation: logical-consistency (divergence detector stale vs Phase 03 schema — mission item b)
recommendation: Update normalizeVaultState to read the real CharacterState fields (s.temp_hp, s.death_saves, s.flags, s.concentrating_on, s.exhaustion_level, s.hit_dice_remaining, s.attunements, s.resources_used, s.xp, s.level) and keep neutral-default pairing only for fields neither side tracks.
verdict_notes: Code defect CONFIRMED by independent reading: normalizeVaultState (src/ai/master/vault/parity-check.ts:158-176) hardcodes vault-side defaults (temp_hp 0, death_saves 0/0, flags all-false, concentrating_on null, exhaustion 0, hit_dice_remaining 0, attunements [], resources_used {}, xp 0, level 1) while CharacterState has tracked all of these since Phase 03 (src/ai/master/vault/projector.ts:130-153; commits c37549f/847467d/2a50d1c). Git ancestry proves parity-check (e2f794c) predates the CharacterState extension and was never updated; the test suite (tests/ai/master/vault/parity-check.test.ts) fixtures only level:1/xp:0 so it cannot catch this. normalizePostgresState (parity-check.ts:198-231) uses live PG values, and snapshot.ts:478 seeds hitDiceRemaining=hitDiceMax non-null at session creation — so under dual-write the asymmetry yields false positives on any dual-written Phase 03 mutation and false negatives on vault-only Phase 03 divergences; tools.ts:388 would feed the divergence text back to the LLM. SEVERITY REFUTED, downgraded high→low: (1) parityCheck only runs via dualWriteApplyEvent, gated on campaign.settings.dualWrite===true (resolveDualWrite, src/lib/preferences.ts:174-177) with deliberately NO env override; (2) live Postgres query shows ZERO campaigns with dualWrite set — including the active One Piece campaign 3ef630db (masterBackend=vault, sourceOfTruth=vault, vaultMutations=true, no dualWrite key); (3) dual_write_divergences table has 0 rows — no audit spam has ever occurred; (4) the live campaign's characters are level 1 / xp 0 anyway. Player impact on the current default path is zero; the code is unreachable. Also one evidence sub-claim is wrong: the seed does NOT populate Phase 03 fields (src/campaigns/seed-vault.ts:95-122 emits only id/name/hp_max/hp_current/spell_slots), so for PG-seeded never-mutated fields (level, hit dice) the vault genuinely sits at defaults and a faithful normalizer would report the same divergence — that part is a true positive against a seed-completeness gap, not a normalizer false positive. The fix recommendation (harvest real CharacterState fields) is still correct and worthwhile before any future dual-write re-enable, but as a latent defect in a currently-dead migration tool it is low severity today.

title: Dual-write has no atomicity and no event-id idempotency: partial failure + LLM retry double-applies state on the surviving leg
evidence: dualWriteApplyEvent runs `Promise.all([EventsWriter.applyEvent(...), applyEngineMutation()])` (dual-writer.ts:131-134); Promise.all rejection does NOT cancel the other leg, and there is no compensation — if the PG leg fails the vault append stays in append-only events.md (and vice versa: the PG UPDATE may have committed when the vault leg fails). The error returns to the LLM as `isError: true` (tools.ts:394), and the standard model behavior is to retry the same apply_event — the retry allocates a NEW envelope id (tools.ts:325) and the surviving leg applies the mutation a second time (e.g. hp_change delta applied twice in the vault, once in PG). The envelope JSDoc claims `id ... Enables idempotent retries` (events-schema.ts:348-349), but nothing dedupes by id: EventsWriter.applyEvent appends unconditionally (events-writer.ts:86-89) and replayEvents never inspects env.id for duplicates (projector.ts:909-955).
rule_citation: logical-consistency (mission items b/e — partial-failure double application)
recommendation: Either sequence the writes (vault first, PG second, and on PG failure append a compensating inverse event) or implement the claimed idempotency: have the LLM-facing dispatcher reuse a deterministic event id per tool_use id and make EventsWriter/replay skip already-seen ids.
verdict_notes: Code claims verified line-by-line and all accurate: dual-writer.ts:131-134 runs Promise.all with no cancellation/compensation; tools.ts:325 allocates a fresh randomUUID per dispatch so an LLM retry after isError (tools.ts:392-395) creates a new event id; events-writer.ts:86-89 appends unconditionally; projector.ts replayEvents (909-955) never dedupes by env.id; the events-schema.ts:347-350 JSDoc claim that id 'Enables idempotent retries' is unimplemented (grep for idempot/dedup across src finds no id-based dedupe; vault/loop.ts duplicate guards only cover suppressCombatMutations encounter-event drops, a different scenario). So the logical gap is real. HOWEVER severity 'high' is wrong for the current default path: resolveDualWrite (src/lib/preferences.ts:174-177) is per-campaign operator opt-in with no env default, and a live read-only listing (pnpm vault:cutover) shows EVERY campaign in the DB has dualWrite=off — active vault campaigns run backend=vault/mut=on/dw=off/sot=vault, i.e. the single-write path at tools.ts:398-417, so the cited Promise.all branch is currently unreachable. Dual-write exists only as a transient migration soak/rollback mechanism (scripts/vault-flip-helpers.ts:345-347; cutover-checklist end state is 'no campaign still dualWrite=on'; decommission-legacy-state.ts blocks on dw=on). Even during a soak, dual-writer.ts's own JSDoc documents partial failure as an accepted operator-remediated divergence, and a retry that succeeds on both legs is caught by the synchronous parityCheck (divergence recorded to dual_write_divergences + surfaced in the tool result), so it is detected, not silent. Residual on the ACTIVE path: if EventsWriter append succeeds but regenerateAffectedViews throws (tools.ts:401-409), an LLM retry double-appends in the vault with no id dedupe — real but requires a local-fs failure between two same-disk operations, far rarer than the PG-failure scenario the finding cites. Recommendation (deterministic per-tool_use event id + replay dedupe) is still worth doing before any future dual-write soak on the prod M4 campaign, but player impact today is negligible → low.

title: Server-resolved combat events bypass the dual-write Postgres leg (dualWrite flag not forwarded)
evidence: The encounter-opener, v1 player-attack resolver, and monster-turn loop all dispatch their authoritative events via `dispatchVaultTool('apply_event', ev, { campaignId, sessionId })` (route.ts:409, 477, 534, 608) — WITHOUT the `dualWrite` flag, which is only forwarded to the LLM loop input (route.ts:745). On a dual-write campaign the dispatcher therefore takes the Phase-02 single-write branch (tools.ts:340 requires ctx.dualWrite===true) for every server-resolved event, including PC `hp_change` from monster attacks. Postgres session_state.hpCurrent never sees server-resolved damage, so the next LLM-emitted dual-written event triggers a parity divergence CAUSED by the system itself, and the dual_write_divergences audit data becomes meaningless. Errors from these dispatches are also only console.warn'd (route.ts:609-611) — a failed monster-damage event is silently dropped.
recommendation: Forward `...(dualWriteEnabled && { dualWrite: true })` plus the resolved characterId at all four server-resolver dispatch sites, or explicitly document/neutralize hp_change in the parity surface for dual-write campaigns.
verdict_notes: Mechanically confirmed: route.ts:409/477/534/608 dispatch apply_event with only {campaignId, sessionId}; tools.ts:340 requires ctx.dualWrite===true, so server-resolved events always take the single-write branch; dualWrite is forwarded only at route.ts:745 (LLM loop). The monster-loop site (608) emits PC hp_change (monster-turns.ts:220) which has a real Postgres mapping (event-to-engine-mutation.ts hp_change -> session_state.hpCurrent) and parity-check diffs hp_current, so on a dualWrite=true campaign server-resolved damage would cause system-generated divergences — the logical-consistency claim holds. HOWEVER severity is overstated: (1) three of four cited sites emit only encounter-scoped events whose Postgres leg is an explicit no-op by design (REQ-037 / event-to-engine-mutation.ts:466-479) and whose parity check is skipped (no payload.character), so forwarding dualWrite there is inert; (2) dualWrite is a transitional migration flag, default false, and a live DB query shows 0 of 135 campaigns with dualWrite=true — the bug is unreachable on the current default path (vault backend, sourceOfTruth=vault, single-write); (3) even in the coexistence window, sourceOfTruth=vault gameplay reads come from events.md so players see correct HP — impact limited to audit pollution and a stale rollback target; (4) the console.warn-only error handling is the documented D-10 design (never hard-fail the turn), not a defect. Real latent inconsistency worth a one-line fix or doc note if the coexistence window is ever reopened, but zero player impact today → low.

title: Postgres dual-write leg writes per-character events onto the single per-session session_state row (multi-PC misrouting)
evidence: session_state is keyed solely by sessionId with no characterId column (session-state.ts:7-9). The module header of event-to-engine-mutation.ts justifies bypassing the applicator precisely because it would 'misroute mutations for events whose payload.character is NOT the host PC in a multi-PC party' (lines 21-23) — yet its own hp_change clamps the TARGET character's hpMax against the SHARED row's hpCurrent and writes back to that shared row (lines 121-125), and condition_add/remove, temp_hp_set, death saves, concentration, exhaustion, and hit dice all do the same (lines 133-163, 233-373). In a multi-PC party, monster damage to PC B decrements the host's session-level HP in Postgres. parityCheck likewise diffs ANY character's vault state against the one shared session_state row (parity-check.ts:121-127), so the comparison is wrong for every non-host character.
recommendation: Scope session-level state per character (add characterId to session_state or a per-character state table) before trusting dual-write/parity data in multi-PC campaigns; at minimum, skip the PG leg and parity-check when payload.character differs from the session's host characterId.
verdict_notes: Code reading CONFIRMED at every cited location: session_state is keyed solely by sessionId (session-state.ts:6-9); invokeEnginePathwayFromEvent's hp_change reads the target character's hpMax but clamps/writes the shared session_state.hpCurrent keyed only on sessionId (event-to-engine-mutation.ts:115-127), and temp_hp/conditions/death-saves/concentration/exhaustion/hit-dice do the same (129-163, 226-373) — directly contradicting the module header's own multi-PC-misrouting rationale (lines 19-25). parityCheck diffs any character's vault state against the one shared session_state row (parity-check.ts:121-127). No compensating guard exists: the dispatcher (tools.ts:311-319, 352-355) only enforces UUID shape on payload.character, not host-PC identity. HOWEVER, severity is overstated: (1) the entire PG leg + parity-check is gated on ctx.dualWrite===true (tools.ts:340), resolved by resolveDualWrite (preferences.ts:174-177, no env override) — I queried the production Supabase DB and 0 of 135 live campaigns have dualWrite=true, including the live One Piece campaign (vault backend, sourceOfTruth=vault); the flagged code is unreachable on the current default path. (2) Dual-write is a temporary migration-window mechanism slated for shutdown (decommission-legacy-state.ts:104-107 blocks decommission until dualWrite is off everywhere). (3) Even if re-enabled with sourceOfTruth=vault, player reads come from vault replay — misrouting would only corrupt the PG rollback safety net and emit spurious divergence audit rows; player-visible corruption requires multi-PC + dualWrite + sourceOfTruth=postgres, which no campaign is in. (4) The misrouting mirrors a documented pre-existing limitation of the whole legacy PG model (snapshot.ts:447-482: per-PC HP/conditions/death-saves live on the per-session row; non-active party members get faked defaults), and the only multi-PC party in production (2 PCs) is on the baked backend without vault mutations. Real latent inconsistency, sound recommendation as a precondition for ever re-enabling dual-write on multi-PC campaigns, but zero player impact today → low.

title: monster_hp_change has no upper clamp: monsters can be over-healed above hpMax indefinitely
evidence: The encounter reducer applies `monster.hpCurrent = Math.max(0, monster.hpCurrent + delta)` with no `Math.min(hpMax, ...)` (projector.ts:870), unlike the PC hp_change arm which clamps to hp_max (projector.ts:308-310). The validator accepts any finite delta including positive and non-integer values (events-schema.ts:1105). An LLM-emitted positive monster_hp_change (plausible for weak-tool models narrating regeneration) raises monster HP above hpMax without bound, prolonging combats; the CombatTracker then renders hpCurrent > hpMax.
rule_citation: data/rules.md §3.21 ('Cannot exceed maximum HP') applied to creatures + logical-consistency
recommendation: Clamp to `Math.min(monster.hpMax, ...)` in the monster_hp_change reducer arm, mirroring the PC hp_change clamp.
verdict_notes: Code claims verified: projector.ts:870 has no upper clamp (Math.max(0, hp+delta) only), unlike the PC hp_change arm at projector.ts:308-311 which clamps to hp_max; events-schema.ts:1105 accepts any finite delta including positive non-integers; combat-reducer.test.ts E11 (lines 60/170) intentionally supports positive deltas (healing dead monsters) with no hpMax-cap test. Rule citation accurate: data/rules.md:278 §3.21 Healing 'Cannot exceed maximum HP.' However, the claimed exploit path is neutralized by layered guards on the current default path: (1) isNarrationOnlyTurn (combat-resolver.ts:98-116) sets offerTools:false for ALL models on every active-encounter turn, combat declaration, roll result, and server-resolved turn — the LLM never has apply_event during combat, and weak-tool models (gemma) are tool-less on EVERY turn via isWeakToolModel (route.ts:724); (2) inline-event text recovery (loop.ts:304-306) is gated on offeredTools.length>0 && !suppressCombatMutations, so leaked events never apply on combat turns; (3) the server resolver emits delta=-total (combat-resolver.ts:308, total parsed non-negative) and the v2 monster loop emits clamped PC hp_change, never positive monster_hp_change; (4) monster_spawn initializes hpCurrent=hpMax (projector.ts:808), so hpCurrent≤hpMax holds on the server-owned path. The only residual trigger is a contrived single tool-enabled non-combat turn where the model spontaneously emits combat_start/monster_spawn plus a positive monster_hp_change (next turn becomes tool-less), or manual events.md edits. The missing clamp is a real invariant gap worth fixing (mirror Math.min(monster.hpMax,...)), but unbounded over-heal / prolonged combat has no realistic path on the current vault+qwen3 default, so severity is low, not medium.

title: hp_change / monster_hp_change deltas accept non-integer values: fractional HP in vault, rounding drift vs Postgres integer columns
evidence: Both validators check only `typeof delta === 'number' && Number.isFinite(delta)` (events-schema.ts:433, 1105) — unlike qty/level/count which require Number.isInteger. A delta of -3.5 yields fractional hp_current in the vault state and view file, while the PG leg writes the same fractional value into the integer column session_state.hp_current (session-state.ts:10), where Postgres' numeric→integer cast rounds — producing a permanent off-by-fraction divergence between layers.
recommendation: Require Number.isInteger(delta) in both hp_change and monster_hp_change validator arms (D&D HP is integral).
verdict_notes: CONFIRMED with one mechanical correction. (1) Validator gap verified: events-schema.ts:433 (hp_change) and :1105 (monster_hp_change) accept any finite number for delta, while every other numeric field (level :474, initiative :1086, qty/tempHp/count/uses/amount per tests) requires Number.isInteger. Test suite has 'rejects non-integer' tests for 8 other fields but zero fractional-delta coverage — oversight, not deliberate. (2) No compensating code: projector.ts:310/:870 add raw delta with clamp only; no rounding anywhere in src/ai/master/vault; tools.ts dispatch adds only a UUID guard; hp_change passes through even on deterministic combat turns (loop.ts:399-404), so a qwen3-emitted delta:-3.5 reaches the vault on the default path. (3) Rulebook holds: data/rules.md:250 'Resistance: take half damage (round down)' and :48 'Halve (round down)' — HP is integral, and resistance halving (7/2=3.5) is a realistic LLM emission. (4) CORRECTION on the PG leg: the claimed 'Postgres rounds → permanent off-by-fraction divergence' is wrong twice over. The PG dual-write leg only runs when campaign.settings.dualWrite===true (resolveDualWrite defaults false, preferences.ts:174-177 — opt-in migration audit mode, NOT the current default), and the driver is node-postgres (src/db/client.ts), which sends 16.5 as text '16.5' that PG's int4 input REJECTS with an error rather than rounding — a dual-write campaign would get a thrown PG-leg error (isError:true to the LLM, fractional event already in the vault), not silent drift. (5) Player impact on current default path (vault single-write, qwen3): fractional hp_current in vault state and materialized view, silently granting the half-point that round-down denies; no crash; self-corrects only at clamp bounds. Severity 'low' is correct. Recommendation (Number.isInteger in both arms) is sound and matches the codebase pattern.
